entry.title: Problems creating a multitenancy app with CAP
entry.content:
Hello,

we want to create a multitenancy CAP app with Approuter and standalone HTML5 apps (html5-repo), but without portal or workzone. An example is linked (https://github.com/christopher-ketzler/book). The deployment (mta) of the app and the creation of the route works fine. Unfortunately, an “Internal Server Error” occurs when the app is called up. The following can be seen in the application router log. The app is not able to authenticate againt the subscriber subaccount. I have no clue why this happens as I changed the tenant-mode to "shared" (see below).

The app was created in the similar as described in the Cap documentation. It was developed on the BAS and deployed on a BTP trial. The xs-security.json has been extended to support multitenancy and the trial account:

"tenant-mode":"shared",

"oauth2-configuration": {

  "redirect-uris": ["https://*.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/**"]

}

The Approuter (xs-app.json) has been extended to include the link to the Fiori Elements app:

"welcomeFile": "bookui",

I have noticed that the uua tenantmode configuration listed in the HTML5 Runtime does not change to “shared” after updating the tenant. Why is this the case?

The project was created as follows:

  • cds init book –add tiny-sample
  • Creation of the Fiori Elements App (Wizzard).
  • cds add hana,xsuaa,approuter,html5-repo
  • cds add multitenancy
  • cds add mta
  • npm i (also within the mtx modul)
  • Adjustments see below.
  • mbt build
  • cf deploy mta_archive/… -f
  • Creation oft he route in the BTP Cockpit.
  • Update the tenant in the “SaaS Provisioning Service“

Regards

Christopher

The latter has a calculated total, but there is not average of all the Ind Percent values in a worksheet. Is there a way to do this? I don't see custom fields having an option to enable a total average either.

Regards,

 

Roman

entry.title: Re: Not able to create sales order with reference of sche...
entry.content:
Hello @pavankumar1127 

The pop up message about update cancellation and the fact that sales orders are not really saved, indicate a serious issue in your sales order processing - sales order saving update tasks fail and that can be seen in SM13.

Most probably sales order saving fails with a short dump. Short dumps can be viewed and analyzed in ST22. You need to find the root cause of the issue. Again most probably it is a problem in some custom development like output determination. That can be found out from the short dumps. 

If you don't know how to analyze short dumps, go to ST22, get to a short dump from sales order saving (transaction VA01), download entire dump into a zip file and attach it here. Then we can say more about the problem.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Mobile Start-Push Notification
entry.content:
By default, the Subject Name Identifier configured in the entry for SAP Build Work Zone under Applications in your Identity Authentication tenant is used by SAP Mobile Services to identify users, e.g. for push notifications. When you check the "Push Registrations" table in SAP Mobile Services, you see the values under "user name". From your provided notification settings in SAP Build Work Zone, you currently have selected "E-Mail (default)" as the identifier to deliver notifications. If in the username column another value (e.g. a UID) would be shown, this is the reason for the error message that no matching registration was found. You can also refer to SAP Note 3582623 for more information.

From there, there are multiple ways to solve this:

  • With SAP Task Center and the end-to-end configuration of the Global User Id, notifications will also make use of the Global User Id.
  • Without Task Center, you could either streamline the Subject Name Identifier to E-Mail or the S/4HANA User Id. This may include the removal of cached shadow-users on your SAP BTP Cockpit.
  • Another way would be to override the matching attribute in SAP Mobile Services.

We would recommend you reaching out to sapmobilestart@sap.com, where me and my colleagues can take a look at your case specifically and recommend the best next steps.

entry.title: Re: Get Column details of a calculation view in HANA
entry.content:
Thanks! I came here after a Google search looking for getting all the column names for a CV. If anyone else wonders this worked the best with the correct column ordering:

 

SELECT
	SCHEMA_NAME,
	CATALOG_NAME,
	CUBE_NAME,
	COLUMN_NAME,
	DESCRIPTION
FROM 
	"_SYS_BI"."BIMC_PROPERTIES"
WHERE 
	CAPTION = 'models.standard::CV_...'
ORDER BY "ORDER"

entry.title: SAP GRC - risk owner stage are triggered before all role owner related stage completes
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Within SAP GRC 12, we have different path at role owner stage to assess the different role owner scenario (e.g: Whether requestor ID is role owner as role owner will not be able to approve their own assignments). And also, a path for roles which are pre-approved (default assigned roles).

However, we encounter an issue where the request would partially redirect to risk owner stage if either one of the mention paths completes.

Referring to the screen shot, the path Z_ROLE_OWNER_BYPASS at role owner stage was approved and it was partially redirected to Z_SOD_REV (risk owner stage), however the other role owner stage path Z_NEW_CHG_ACCT is still yet completed where the resulted risks could be removed if one of the role owner rejects the assignment.

The expected outcome is that the risk owner stage should only trigger upon the completion of all role owner stage, which would then present the accurate access risks. Could any experts kindly share whether this can be achieved in standard configurations?  

Thank you.

entry.title: EBS – Outgoing payment automatic clearing
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

On S/4HANA Public Cloud outgoing payments processed in SAP are not automatically cleared when Bank Statement is imported.

When the outgoing payment is created in the DMEE file, xml structure the number of payment document is available in the field End to End ID in form HR00##########.

In the SSCUI 101024 Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement Posting rule F004 and interpretation algorithm 020 – Document Number are assigned to External transaction.

In SSCUI 103635 Define Parameter Sets Document number range is assigned.

But when the bank statement is imported no document is found and automatic clearing is not performed.

In App Manage Processing Rule Action Types AP/AR Posting (for subledger clearing) or Clear in Sequence are available. Additionally conditions are set: Memo line contains HR00 and external transaction. Additionally substring logic that would extract document number is missing (like substring start position 5 (to skip HR00) and length 10 or search string mapping HR00########## -> ##########).

What is correct approach to set substring in Manage Processing Rule to extract correct document number?

Kind regards,

Slađana Lučić

entry.title: Consumption Based Planning in Sourcing and Procurement - Part 1
entry.content:
A) What is MRP?

The central role of Material Requirements Planning (MRP) is to monitor stocks and, in particular, to automatically generate procurement proposals for purchasing and production, such as planned orders, purchase requisitions, or delivery schedule lines.

Two main types of planning include Consumption Based Planning and Deterministic Planning (a.k.a MRP). Deterministic Planning is for current or future demands based on Planned Independent Requirements and is planned for the entire structure of Bill of Materials. On the other hand, Consumption Based Planning uses historical consumption values to calculate future demands. The type of planning triggers the net requirement calculation when the current stock + planned fixed receipts (Fixed PR, PO) falls below the requirement stock. Consumption-based planning consists of material planning procedures that are relatively easy to implement. These planning procedures are used in areas without in-house production and/or in production plants to plan B and C parts and operating supplies. B and C parts are typically externally procured and are low value materials with low fluctuating consumptions.

B) Defining of the Planning Procedure for a Material

The MRP Type is a field in the material master record. One of the most important functions of the MRP type is to define the planning method for a material. SAP S/4HANA provides predefined MRP types.

C) There are 3 types of Consumption Based Planning.

  • Reorder Based Planning triggers the net requirement calculation when the current stock + planned fixed receipts (Fixed PR, PO) falls below the automatic or manual reorder point.
  • In case Forecast Based Planning, future values are calculated based on historical consumption. Until now, MRP Type VV was used. However, as per SAP Compatibility Package, the MRP type expires by December 31st, 2025. In its place, the MRP type PD copies the forecast values as Planned Independent Requirements which is also same as Deterministic Planning.
  • Third type is Time Phase planning which also calculates future requirements based on historical consumption. However, the planning run is scheduled to run at predefined intervals.

D) What is Reorder Type Planning?

The basis of reorder point planning is the comparison of available stock with the reorder point. If the available stock is less than the reorder point, the difference represents a shortage and procurement is triggered. The reorder point is defined as the expected material requirement during the replenishment lead time (Planned Delivery Time + Goods Receipt Processing Days + Purchase Processing Time) plus the safety stock.

The following figure illustrates the idea of reorder point: When the available stock reaches the reorder point, a procurement proposal is created. Stock continues to be withdrawn during the replenishment lead time. Normally, the safety stock should not have been taken when the ordered quantity is received. The new available quantity will last for some time until the stock reaches the reorder point again.

E) Lot Size Calculation:

The net requirements calculation determines the shortage quantities for the respective requirements dates. These shortage quantities must be covered by order proposals. Lot-size calculation is the process of calculating the order proposal quantities, which are not necessarily equal to the shortage quantities.

Static Lot Sizing Procedures:

  1. EX – Lot for Lot Order Qty means that the order quantity exactly matches the requirement quantity.

Example:

MRP Type: VB

Lot Sizing Procedure: EX

Current Stock: 0

Reorder Point : 80

Maximum Lot Size : 50

Minimum Lot Size : 46

80 is demand. Since the min lot size is 46, 2 PR is generated with 50+46 (minimum lot size) = 96 qty.

       2. FS – Fixed and Splitting means

  • The system always generates procurement or production orders of a fixed, constant quantity — the lot size you specify.
  • This means that no matter what the exact requirement is, SAP will create orders in multiples of the fixed lot size.

Example:

MRP Type: VB

Lot Sizing Procedure: FS

Current Stock: 0

Reorder Point : 80

Maximum / Minimum Lot Size : Nil

80 is demand. Since the fixed lot size is 35 and rounding profile is 7 (multiple of fixed lot size), 15 PRs is generated with 7 qty each with total of 105 qty.

         3. FX – Fixed Order Qty means the order quantity is always the fixed lot size value you specify, regardless of the actual requirement.

Example:

MRP Type: VB

Lot Sizing Procedure: FX

Current Stock: 0

Reorder Point : 80

Maximum / Minimum Lot Size : Nil

80 is demand. Since the fixed lot size is 34, 3 PR is generated with 34*3 = 102 qty.

         4. HB - Replenish up to maximum stock level. When you use HB, SAP calculates the order quantity so that the stock level after procurement reaches the predefined maximum stock level.

Example:

MRP Type: VB

Lot Sizing Procedure: FX

Current Stock: 0

Reorder Point : 80

Maximum / Minimum Lot Size : Nil

Maximum Stick Level : 81

80 is demand. Since the max lot level is 81, 1 PR is generated with 81 qty.

Note: Periodic Lot Sizing Procedure will be covered in Part 2 in Forecast Planning Procedure.

F) Rounding:

  • Rounding Value: If you have maintained a rounding value, the system rounds the calculated quantity to the multiple of this value.
  • Rounding Profile:  Using a rounding profile allows for scaled rounding. Rounding profiles must be configured individually and assigned in a material master record.

A rounding profile consists of threshold values and rounding values. The threshold is the value at which the system rounds up. The rounding value is the value to which the system rounds up when the threshold is exceeded.

If the requirement value exceeds the first threshold, the system rounds up to the next multiple of the rounding value found. If the requirement value falls below the first threshold, the system uses the original requirement value.

Threshold Value

Rounding Value

2

5

32

40

 

Calculated order quantity before rounding

Order quantity after rounding

Explanation

1

1

The requirement value is less than the first threshold (2). That's why the system does not round up.

2

5

The first threshold is reached. The system rounds up to 5.

6

10

5 is included in 6. For the remaining 1 piece, the system rounds up to 5. This results in a procurement quantity of 10 pieces.

7

10

5 is included in 7. For the remaining 2 pieces, the system rounds up to 5. This results in a procurement quantity of 10 pieces.

21

25

5 is included 4 times in 21. For the remaining 1 piece, the system rounds up to 5. This results in an order quantity of 25.

31

35

5 is included 6 times in 31. For the remaining 1 piece, the system rounds up to 5. This results in an order quantity of 35.

32

40

The second threshold is reached. The system rounds up to 40.

41

45

40 is included once in 41. For the remaining 1 piece, the system rounds up to 5. This results in an order quantity of 45 pieces.

74

80

40 is included once in 80. For the remaining 34 pieces, the system rounds up to 40. This results in an order quantity of 80 pieces.

G) Scheduling:

What is scheduling? It means calculating the basic date of the procurement proposal. (i.e the date on which the PO must be created, the date on which the goods must be received and the date on which goods will be available for use)

There are 3-time elements in scheduling:

  1. Planned Delivery Time in MRP Tab of Material Master (The planned delivery time is the number of calendar days required to procure the material via external procurement. The planned delivery time is defined in the material master record at plant level. You can also store the planned delivery time in outline agreements or purchasing info records.)
  1. Goods Receipt Processing Days in MRP Tab of Material Master (The goods receipt processing time is the time in working days between the receipt of the material and its availability in the warehouse. It is needed, for example, to unpack, check, and place the material into storage. The goods receipt processing time is maintained in the material master record at plant level or in outline agreements.)

If the system can determine a source of supply (info record or outline agreement) during the planning run, the planned delivery time and the goods receipt processing time that may exist in this source of supply are given priority in scheduling over the values in the material master record.

         3. Processing Time for Purchasing in customizing in Consumption Based Planning along the following SPRO path Materials Management → Consumption-Based Planning → Plant Parameters → Carry Out Overall Maintenance of Plant Parameters -> External Procurement

In Forecast Planning, the scheduling done is called Backward Scheduling. In Reorder Point Planning, we follow Forward Scheduling.

In Backward Scheduling, the requirement dates in the future is known. i.e the material must be available on this date. GR Processing Time is first subtracted to identify the date when the goods should be received on premise. Then, Planned Delivery Time is then subtracted to determine when the Purchase Order must be released to vendor. Then, Purchasing Processing Time is subtracted which allows us to arrive at Purchase Requisition Release Date.

If in case the requirement date is in past during Forecast Based Scheduling, the system switches to Forward Scheduling.

Example:

MRP Type: PD

Purchase Processing Time = 10 Days

Planned Delivery Time = 10 Days

Goods Receipt Processing Days = 3 Days

Forecast Schedule / Material should be available for use date = 1st October, 2025

Delivery Date = 1st October – 3 Days = 28th September , 2025

PO Release Date to Vendor = 28th September – 10 Days = 18th September, 2025

PR Release Date = 18th September = 10 Days = 8th September, 2025

PR Creation Date = Today (28th June, 2025, the day on which MRP is run)

In Forward Scheduling during Reorder Point, procurement should be started immediately if the stock falls below the reorder point. The date on which stock falls below reorder point is taken as PR release date. Then subsequent dates are added to arrive at goods availability date.

Example:

MRP Type: VB

Lot Size Procedure : HB

Reorder Point : 10000

Maximum Lot Size : 10000

Available Qty: 4028

Expected PR Qty via MRP Run: 10000 – 4028 = 5972.

Purchase Processing Time = 99 Days

Planned Delivery Time = 5 Days

GR Processing Days = 2 Days

PR Creation Date = Today (28th June , 2025)

PR Release Date = Today (28th June, 2025)

PO Release Date to Vendor = 28th June + 99 Days = 5th October, 2025

Delivery Date = 5th October + 5 Days = 10th October, 2025

Available Date = 10th October + 2 Days = 12th October, 2025

H) Procurement Proposals:

Material requirements planning detects shortages and automatically generates procurement proposals for purchasing and production. Procurement Proposals can be 3 types:

Planned Orders

Purchase Requisitions

Schedule Agreement – Schedule Lines

The system looks at MRP in material master. If the Procurement Type is X (Both) or E (In House Production), the planning tool generates Planned Orders as procurement proposals. In case of F (External Procurement) as Procurement Type, the planning tool generates Purchase Requisitions and Schedule Lines as procurement proposals.

In the case of external procurement, it tries to find a valid source of supply among those available in the system (contract, scheduling agreement, or info record). First the planning tool tries to check if the validity date of the source of supply covers the planned delivery date of the procurement proposal and then checks if the source of supply is relevant for planning.

Scheduling Agreement has the highest priority is source of supply. If there are multiple active agreements, system checks for released agreements. If there are multiple release agreements, system checks for agreement with lowest document number.

Purchase Contract has second highest priority as source of supply. If there are multiple active and released contracts, system prioritises plant specific contracts have higher priority than cross plant contracts. If there are multiple plant specific contracts, system checks for contracts with lowest document number.

Info Record is chosen as source of supply only if automatic sourcing is checked. Otherwise it is ignored. Plant specific info record has higher priority than purchase organisation level info record. Then lowest supplier number. Then lowest purchase organisation number. Then, lowest purchasing group number.

If the basic source of supply does not fulfil your requirements, you can use source list to override the above for a particular validity period or use quota arrangement to use multiple sources of supply at the same time.

I) MRP Areas

An MRP Area defines a planning scope within a plant for which Material Requirements Planning (MRP) can be executed independently—such as specific storage locations or subcontractors—rather than the entire plant. Working with MRP areas has its advantages: Using MRP areas for storage locations and subcontractors allows differentiation in planning. You can plan materials separately for each MRP area. By defining specific MRP areas, you can carry out precise requirements planning for these areas. The use of MRP areas allows for more timely material provisioning in appropriate quantities, for example, for a specific production line, a spare parts location or a subcontractor's stock.

Types of MRP Areas

  1. Plant MRP Area (Type 01)
    • Automatically created for every plant.
    • Covers all storage locations and subcontractor stock unless overridden
    • Default System Configuration
  2. Storage Location MRP Area (Type 02)
    • Customized grouping of one or more storage locations.
    • If assigned, MRP runs only for that set of locations; they’re excluded from the plant-level MRP area.
    • One MRP Area of Type Storage Location can be assigned to one or more storage locations as well. This is not possible in MRP Area of Type Subcontractor. Only one to one mapping is possible.
  3. Subcontractor MRP Area (Type 03)
    • Created per vendor/subcontractor.
    • Plans component provisioning to subcontractors independently

Example:

MRP Area = Plant

MRP Type: VB

Reorder Point : 100

Lot Sizing Procedure : HB

Maximum Lot Size: 100

Stock on Hand at Storage Location RXXX – 10 (No MRP Area maintained)

Stock on Hand at Storage Location FDXX – 10 (No MRP Area maintained)

Total Stock on Hand at Plant Level - 20

PR created for 80 Qty during MRP Run at Plant Level

MRP Area = FRXX Storage Location Level

MRP Type: VB

Reorder Point : 200

Lot Sizing Procedure : HB

Maximum Lot Size: 200

Stock on Hand at Storage Location RXXX – 10

Stock on Hand at Storage Location FXXX – 10

PR created for 200 Qty during MRP Run at FRXX MRP Area Level (irrespective of plant level stock)

J) Other Re-order Planning MRP types:

VB - Manual Reorder Point is a MRP Procedure where both the reorder point and safety stock are manually maintained in the material master record. The system does not automatically calculate these values; When the available stock (including current inventory and firmed receipts) drops below the reorder point, the system triggers procurement proposals to replenish the stock but taking only lot size / reorder point as demand. It does not consider any reservations / sales orders / PIRs into account. Examples have been shown in multiple cases in the above sections of the blog.

VM - Automatic Reorder Point is an MRP procedure where SAP automatically calculates both the reorder point and safety stock using historical consumption data and forecast values.  When the available stock (including current inventory and firmed receipts) drops below the auto-calculated reorder point, SAP triggers procurement proposals like planned orders or purchase requisitions. It does not consider sales orders, PIRs, or reservations in this calculation.

MRP Type: VM

Service Level: 99.9%  (Service Level a percentage reflecting the proportion of demand you aim to fulfill directly from stock-on-hand during lead time. A service level of, say, 99% implies you want enough buffer to meet demand 95% of the time without running out )

Planned Delivery Time = 10 Days

Goods Processing Days = 3 Days

Purchasing Processing Time = 0 Days

Historical periods: 3

Forecast period: 3

Consumption Values: (67+89+78) / 3 = 78

Total replenishment Lead Time = Planned delivery time + GR processing time + Processing time required by purchasing

Reorder Point = Safety Stock + Forecast consumption * Replenishment Lead Time / Forecast period

Total Replenishment lead Time = 10 * 5/7 (5 working days out of 7 in a week) + 3 + 0 = 10.14 Days

Forecast Period = 90 * 5/7 = 64.26 Days

Reorder Point = 234 * 10.14 / 64.26 = 36.9018

MRP 1 View

Forecasting View:

Maintain Historical Consumption for 3 months in MRP2 View and give execute forecast. Forecast for next 3 periods is shown below and reorder point is calculated accordingly. In case, Service level % is maintained, Safety Stock is also calculated during execution of forecast.

V1 and V2 (Manual / Automatic Reorder Point Planning with External Requirements) : These types are similar to VB and VM but include external requirements like sales orders and reservations in the planning process. The key difference lies in how external requirements are considered during the planning horizon.

In above case of VM, the MRP plans only 33 qty as per reorder point. It is not considering the open reservation with 100 qty.

However, in case of V2 , the planning tool takes 100 qty open reservation as external requirements and plans this into the PR qty as 133.

K) MRP Live

Traditionally, MRP runs as a background job overnight, planning all changed materials in one or more plants. To calculate the material shortage for each material, the system reads all relevant planning elements such as sales orders, purchase orders, and production orders from the database. This means that MRP has to read large amounts of data from the database, and due to this fact and the complex algorithms executed during the planning run, MRP can take a long time.

When SAP developed the SAP HANA database, it offered a way to improve MRP performance by leveraging the power of SAP HANA's in-memory parallelization. This led to the introduction of MRP Live on HANA (also known as MRP Live), an MRP run optimized for SAP HANA.

In SAP S/4HANA, MRP Live on HANA is enabled by default and is the recommended solution for material requirements planning. However, traditional classic MRP transactions are still available. In addition to performance, MRP Live on HANA offers other benefits, such as a new transaction and app with more flexible selection criteria that replaces all classic MRP transactions.

Finally, MRP Live on HANA has been defined by SAP as the future architecture, which means that all innovations in this area will be based on MRP Live and not on classic MRP.

Classic MRP is still available. When does it make sense to use Classic MRP instead of MRP Live?

MRP Live on HANA still has some limitations.

MRP Live is designed for high performance, but does not yet support all existing business processes supported by traditional MRP. For example, some lot-sizing techniques, such as optimal lot-sizing, are not supported. MRP Live on HANA also does not support forecast-based planning or time-phased planning. Current restrictions of MRP Live on HANA are detailed in the SAP Note 1914010. You should take advantage of MRP Live on HANA wherever possible.

Use MRP Live on HANA to start MRP runs. The system checks which materials can be planned with MRP Live and which must be planned the old way. The latter are automatically forwarded to classic planning.

Note:

If you want the system to create planned orders for externally procured materials, you must start your planning run with classic MRP.  To perform a total planning run for a specific plant or MRP area, use transaction MD01 in your backend system. To carry out a single-item planning run for a single material, use transaction MD03.


Learning Source : https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/using-consumption-based-planning-in-sap-s-4hana/introducing-consumption-based-planning

entry.title: Employee Time Report as Individual Line Item - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction:

This Report is all about a requirement from one from my clients where they wanted a Leave Report as an individual Line item rather than the aggregated form. It is also to be remembered that they do not own time tracking license and Story Reports are not enabled either.

How was the Requirement Achieved?

The Lack of Time Tracking License made things interesting and challenging. With time tracking license, Report fetched using Recorded time data v2 will deliver report with individual line items rather than the aggregated form. To better understand this, lets us take a case where an employee applies leave from Jan 01 - Jan 05, The Report fetched using Canva Reporting will deliver the output as Start Date Jan 01 and End Date Jan 05, which will not serve our purpose. The Expected result should be as follows:

 

Jan 01 Annual Leave
Jan 02 Annual Leave
Jan 03 Annual Leave
Jan 04 Annual Leave
Jan 05 Annual Leave

It was also confirmed by the Engineering Team of SAP, that this will not be feasible by Canva Reporting. So, I initially had a thought if this can be achieved using excel using VBA's which did not deliver output as expected. Then I started exploring open-source solutions and there I arrived at a solution using Python. Using Python, I input the file extracted from SAP SuccessFactors which would then split the records individually. However, the Headers mentioned in the Code should match with the Extracted Report from Sap SuccessFactors.

Python Code : 

import pandas as pd
from datetime import timedelta
from tkinter import Tk
from tkinter.filedialog import askopenfilename, asksaveasfilename
 
def split_leave_records():
    # Use tkinter to open a file dialog to select the input Excel file
    Tk().withdraw()  # Close the root Tk window
    file_path = askopenfilename(title="Select the Excel file containing aggregated leave records",
                                filetypes=[("Excel files", "*.xlsx *.xls")])
   
    if not file_path:
        print("No file selected.")
        return
 
    # Read the Excel file
    df = pd.read_excel(file_path)
 
    # Strip whitespace from column names and make them lowercase
    df.columns = df.columns.str.strip().str.lower()
 
    # Print the column names to check for the correct names
    print("Available columns in the file:", df.columns)
 
    # Dynamically check for the presence of expected columns
    required_columns = ['start date', 'end date', 'employee id', 'employee name',
                        'time type', 'approval status', 'quantity in days',
                        'department', 'position', 'application date', 'created by']
    missing_columns = [col for col in required_columns if col not in df.columns]
   
    if missing_columns:
        print(f"Error: Missing columns in the input file: {', '.join(missing_columns)}")
        return
 
    # Convert 'start date' and 'end date' to datetime
    df['start date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['start date'], errors='coerce')
    df['end date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['end date'], errors='coerce')
 
    # Check for invalid dates
    if df['start date'].isnull().any() or df['end date'].isnull().any():
        print("Error: Some 'start date' or 'end date' values could not be parsed.")
        return
 
    # Initialize a list to hold split records
    split_records = []
   
    # Iterate over each row to split the leave records
    for index, row in df.iterrows():
        employee_id = row['employee id']
        employee_name = row['employee name']
        start_date = row['start date']
        end_date = row['end date']
        time_type = row['time type']
        approval_status = row['approval status']
        quantity_in_days = row['quantity in days']
        department = row['department']  # This may be blank
        position_title = row['position']  # Renamed from position
        application_date = row['application date']
        created_by = row['created by']
 
        # Ensure start_date <= end_date
        if start_date > end_date:
            print(f"Error: Start date is after End date for Employee ID {employee_id}")
            continue
 
        # Generate individual records for each date in the range
        current_date = start_date
        day_count = 0
       
        while current_date <= end_date:
            split_records.append({
                'Application Date': application_date.date() if pd.notnull(application_date) else '',  # Keep only date part
                'Employee ID': employee_id,
                'Employee Name': employee_name,
                'Leave Date': current_date.date(),  # Keep only date part
                'Department': department,  # Leave blank if there's no data
                'Position Title': position_title,  # Renamed column
                'Time Type': time_type,
                'Quantity in Days': 1,      # Each split leave is considered 1 day
                'Approval Status': approval_status,
                'Created By': created_by
            })
            current_date += timedelta(days=1)
            day_count += 1
       
        # Warn if the number of days split doesn't match the quantity
        if day_count != quantity_in_days:
            print(f"Warning: Day count ({day_count}) doesn't match expected quantity ({quantity_in_days}) for Employee ID {employee_id}")
 
    # Create a DataFrame for the split records
    split_df = pd.DataFrame(split_records)
 
    # Reorder columns for the final output and remove 'Start Date' and 'End Date'
    split_df = split_df[[
        'Application Date', 'Employee ID', 'Employee Name', 'Leave Date',
        'Department', 'Position Title', 'Time Type',
        'Quantity in Days', 'Approval Status', 'Created By'
    ]]
 
    # Use tkinter to save the output Excel file
    output_file = asksaveasfilename(defaultextension=".xlsx",
                                    filetypes=[("Excel files", "*.xlsx *.xls")],
                                    title="Save the individual leave records to Excel file")
   
    if output_file:
        # Export the split records to a new Excel file
        split_df.to_excel(output_file, index=False)
        print(f"Individual leave records saved to {output_file}")
    else:
        print("Save operation cancelled.")
 
# Run the function to split leave records
split_leave_records()
 
Please Ensure Latest version of Python is downloaded and installed in your System. There are multiple online sources to help you with the installation.
   
Sample Report with Headers :
 
Application DateEmployee IDEmployee NameStart DateEnd DateDepartmentTime TypeQuantity in DaysApproval StatusCreated ByPosition
7-2-202511111111Joseph Mark7-8-20257-27-2025Security  Annual Leave14PENDING2222222

Branch Head

 

Report as Individual Line Items:

Application DateEmployee IDEmployee NameLeave DateDepartmentPosition TitleTime TypeQuantity in DaysApproval StatusCreated By
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-08SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-09SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-10SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-11SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-12SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-13SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-14SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-15SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-16SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-17SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-18SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-19SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-20SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-21SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-22SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-23SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-24SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-25SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-26SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222
2025-07-021111111Joseph Mark2025-07-27SecurityBranch HeadAnnual Leave1PENDING22222222

Just Download the Report from the SAP SuccessFactors with the headers mentioned above , Run the Code in any IDE , Select the file and You will have the report ready in the desired format.

Please remove the Exported to Excel on Date Header from the file after export.

Conclusion:

This explains how open source together with Sap SuccessFactors can do wonders, without the need of Time Tracking and Story Reports enablement.

Feel Free to drop your comments and Thoughts!

Let's make SuccessFactors, A Great and most Preferred HRMS!

entry.title: Re: Handling Field Length Differences in SAP Ariba – SAP ERP including S/4 HANA Integration Using CI
entry.content:
Thanks @Melanie_de_Wit for the feedback on my comments (not everyone on SAP side is taking the time to always answer).

Concerning your feedback, also I have heard it a lot of time and let me try to give my customer feedback (but it is also the same feedback I have heard from a lot of customers):

- "the Ariba solution long pre-dates the purchase by SAP. To change the existing code base and data model would negatively impact any existing customers and configurations performed over time" --> this is just a excuse I have heard a lot of time in my point of view... Of course, it is important to not hurt the existing logic/configuration customers has done  in the past but there is a ton of technical ways to create a technical design  to accommodate the "old way" and a new way at the same time. This technical way can be of course not simple and required some advanced architect to design it but it is totally do-able. It is just very frustrating that nobody on your side is willing to tackle this since a very long time and it is pushing the cost/complexity to your customers....

- "That's not to say that SAP Ariba Procurement will never change!" + "These solutions will evolve into new product offerings" + "SAP ODM"  -> Again this is very nice to hear that some new product version or new products are coming with a better management of those issues but we have been hearing this since years (3to5 years minimum) now and nothing is changing. This is very frustrating and also it's nice that you are building new solutions but the cost to migrate to those others solutions are very high for us, your customers.. What we want is a solution in our current platforms where we have invested millions of euro to implement and deploy worldwide...

entry.title: Re: SAP API PROXY principal propagation error 403
entry.content:
Usually there are more details in the HTTP response body. So what does it say? (For some strange reason, nobody today looks at the body, when they get a non-200 HTTP status code, but especially in error-cases, the body is more important than the code.)

In case of an authentication problem (like Principal Propagation not working), you would get a 401, not a 403. So probably something else is wrong. Two common reasons for 403:

  • You forgot to add the URL to the Access Control List of the SCC. (The SCC then returns a 403, without forwarding the request to the backend.)
  • The user was able to log in ok, but doesn't have the necessary authorization roles to execute the requested URL. (The backend then returns 403.)

But again, the HTTP body will tell you the reason.

entry.title: Re: Apply header-level freight split to line items based...

Dear MK, For the requirement you mentioned: "The requirement is when we input 2,100 THB at the header level, it should be automatically divided by the total quantity to get the freight per unit, and then multiplied by the quantity at each line item accordingly." I think that you only want the system distribute the freight according to the item quantity. If the understanding is correct, you can consider to use the BADI 

Custom Logic: Condition Base Value | SAP Help Portal

to set the condition base value as the item quantity.

In this way, the system will distribute the freight according the quantity.

Best Regards,

Joey

entry.title: Reading child entity in parent entity's determination doesn't work
entry.content:
I am trying to update the total number of items in child entity (Material) in a field in parent entity (Catalogue), however, the read statment returns empty rows for the child entity. How can I fix this, I want to get the count of all the rows in the child entity and update this number in a field in parent entity. Following is the behavior definition and implementation. Thanks.

managed implementation in class zbp_i_catalogue unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zI_catalogue //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zcatalogue
draft table zcatalogue_dr
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( global )
{

  create ( authorization : global );
  update;
  delete;

  field ( readonly, numbering : managed ) CatalogueId;
  field ( readonly ) CategoryDescription, Active, TotalItems, Version,
  CreatedAt, CreatedBy, LastChangedAt, LastChangedBy;

  association _materials { create; with draft; }

  determination saveTotalItems on save { create; }

  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;

  mapping for zcatalogue
    {
      CatalogueId         = catalogue_id;
      Name                = name;
      Description         = description;
      Category            = category;
      CategoryDescription = category_description;
      Active              = active;
      TotalItems          = total_items;
      Version             = version;
      ValidFrom           = valid_from;
      ValidTo             = valid_to;
      LastChangedAt       = last_changed_at;
      LastChangedBy       = last_changed_by;
      CreatedBy           = created_by;
      CreatedAt           = created_at;
    }
}

define behavior for zI_material //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zmaterial
draft table zmaterial_dr
lock dependent by _catalogue
authorization dependent by _catalogue
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly, numbering : managed ) MaterialId;
  field ( readonly ) CatalogueId, CatalogueName, CreatedAt, CreatedBy, LastChangedAt, LastChangedBy;
  
  association _catalogue { with draft; }

  mapping for zmaterial
    {
      MaterialId    = material_id;
      CatalogueId   = catalogue_id;
      CatalogueName = catalogue_name;
      Description   = description;
      Price         = price;
      Currency      = currency;
      Uom           = uom;
      LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
      LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
      CreatedBy     = created_by;
      CreatedAt     = created_at;
    }
}
CLASS lhc_zI_catalogue DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS saveTotalItems FOR DETERMINE ON SAVE
      IMPORTING keys FOR zI_catalogue~saveTotalItems.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_zI_catalogue IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD saveTotalItems.

    READ ENTITIES OF zI_catalogue IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY zI_material
        ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #(
            FOR key IN keys (
                CatalogueId = key-CatalogueId
            )
        )
        RESULT DATA(lt_materials).

    IF lt_materials IS NOT INITIAL.

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF zI_catalogue IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zI_catalogue
      UPDATE FIELDS ( TotalItems )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR _key IN keys ( %tky = _key-%tky TotalItems = lines( lt_materials ) ) ).

    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Employee Central - Automatic Job Relationship Record Creation
entry.content:
Introduction :

In this Blog, I am explaining in detail about how we can make the best use of intelligent services and integration center to automate the process of Job Relationship creation during New Hire.

Details of the Configurations:

Often the maintenance of Job Relationship is missed during new hire creation, and this impacts the workflows and also the PMGM process where it is dependent on the relationships maintained. This Gap can be addressed by leveraging the integration center and intelligent services (ISC) (Thanks to ISC's Event Driven Capability).

Relationship Type :

Relationship Type Label is to be mentioned in the Default Value field , you can check for the Relationship defined under Picklist Center -> jobRelType

 

Event Date :

Event Date is to be mapped to Job Information Start Date

User ID :

Map The User ID to the User ID from Job Information

ReluserID :

RelUserID can either be mentioned in the Default Value or mapped through the calculated Filed if you want it to assign based on conditions.I have mapped it based on the company of the Employee.

and we are all set with the configurations in the Integration Center. Repeat the above steps if you want to maintain Mutiple Job Relationships!

Next is to assign the Integration to the Employee Hire Event which can be done from the Intelligent Services Center.

Please verify if your integration is shown in the Flow

In this case, I am maintaining 2 Relationships (Matrix Manager and HR Manager).

To make all this count, Assigning the Trigger rule is the most important and crucial Task.

Go to Manage Business Configuration -> Assign the Trigger Rule under Job Information 

SAP_EVENT_HIRE is a predelivery event for the ISC by SAP and it is advised not to modify the Rule

Hire a New Employee and See the Magic of Integration in Action!

 

 

Check the Source and this record has been inserted through the Integration Center (API)

Conclusion : 

By leveraging Intelligent Services and the Integration Center, we can seamlessly automate the creation of Job Relationships during the new hire process. This not only eliminates manual steps but also ensures critical relationships like Matrix Manager and HR Manager are consistently and accurately maintained from day one.

Key Benefits:

  • ✅ Improved Data Accuracy – Reduces errors due to missed relationships during hiring.

  • ⚙️ Process Automation – Saves time and effort for HR teams by automating repetitive tasks.

  • 🔄 Workflow Optimization – Ensures downstream processes like workflows and PMGM function smoothly.

  • 📈 Better Compliance & Governance – Maintains standardization across the organization.

Feel Free to Drop your Thoughts and Comments!

entry.title: How to Use Custom Email Addresses from a Z-Table for Invoice Output in OPD?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m working on an output scenario where the recipient email address for invoice output should be determined from a custom Z-table. This table contains (among other things) an email address for each possible invoice recipient.

To make this work, I’ve extended the relevant CDS view (C_BILLINGDOCUMENTOMPARAMDET) using a custom field that reads the email address from the Z-table. In the OPD (Output Parameter Determination), I’m able to select this custom field and assign it to the email recipient parameter.

The issue: although the CDS view field is correctly filled with the email address, the invoice output is still generated without any recipient email address. My suspicion is that the output is being generated before the CDS field is populated, meaning the value is not yet available when the output is triggered.

Is there a supported way to fetch recipient email addresses from a Z-table for invoice output?

Is it possible to delay or control the timing of output generation to ensure the email field is populated beforehand?

Or is there a completely different (preferred) method for managing custom recipient email addresses?

 

(I’m aware of the possible expression "Add email recipients from custom logic" in the Public Cloud version of OPD, but unfortunately this is not available in our Private Cloud environment.)

Any insights or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP API PROXY principal propagation error 403
entry.content:
Well, every HTTP server returns a body with error details... The problem is only, that most HTTP clients (in this case APIM?!) "ignore" the body in case of an error and don't display or trace it....

In the SCC, set the Security Audit Log to "All". Then the SCC will write an audit log entry for every request it receives (accepted or denied). Then, if there is nothing in the audit log, you have the prove that it doesn't reach the SCC.

But I have no idea, what other component could then generate a 403?! You would have to open a ticket for BC-CP-CON to get the Connectivity Service logs checked.

entry.title: Charging of service items to additional service providers in SAP S/4 HANA Transportation Management
entry.content:
This blog post will help readers, customers, and SAP TM users familiarize themselves with the SAP Transportation Management’s functionality to carry out execution of additional services and associated payments in S/4 HANA 2023.

In this blog post I will be shedding light on carrying out additional service payment by eliminating the need to create additional documents thus reducing process complexity. The process may vary on the business needs and situational in nature.

You can read more about SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management -Settlement in the Application Help for SAP S/4HANA

Settlement | SAP Help Portal

Note: I have carried out the demonstration based on my personal tests, observations, insights in S/4HANA 2023 release with relevant screen shot from an internal system for ease of understanding. There’s no relevance or relation to any external source.

Use Case

Use case is for both the customers in Transportation & Logistics Industry (T&L) as well as shippers from Retail, CPG, manufactures etc. operating via Road and have to pay Additional fees for services incurred during or before executing the journey e.g. cleaning, fumigation or insurance etc over and above the contracted charges to an external party. The nature of the charge may be incidental in nature and not applicable for moves.

Assumption: 

The customer is a Manufacturing company and has implemented SAP S/4HANA 2023 and uses embedded SAP Transportation Management.

They have yearly contracts with the multiple road carriers to move certain capacities on a given corridor/ route. Alternatively, they have transportation network across different geographics which involves multiple entry and exists at the border crossing posts involving fees associated towards documentation/processing fees etc. As mentioned earlier this is applicable only if the truck crosses the border here by making it extremely important to track such services and associated charges.   The charges may vary depending on the border crossing location as each border crossing is managed by external parties. Contract in the SAP TM world should be referred as FRA (Freight Agreement).

Challenge:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2022 and lower releases supported such a scenario by creating an additional document termed as a service order which was linked to the associated freight order.
  • A service order as mentioned involves a cumbersome task to process it manually or an enhancement to automate the service order creation and associated settlement.

Solution:

SAP TM now has an out of the box solution to enable charge calculation and settlement for a service incurred in a freight order depending on the location and time of the service. This eliminates the need to create an additional document i.e. the service order and also the stage alternation (Intermediate stop) in the freight order isn’t required to identify the border crossing location.

 (*Depending on the scenarios, Service orders can be leveraged and its significance should be evaluated on a case-by-case basis)

Note: There are multiple ways in which the additional agreement party can be determined be it via std partner determination profile config or via an available BADI, and purely based on the business rule.

Scenario:
A manufacturer is moving goods from its facility in Canada to a customer in US and can be routed via a border crossing across three different points i.e.

  1. Fort Erie
  2. Niagara Falls – Rainbow Bridge
  3. Niagara Falls – Whirlpool Rapids Bridge

Border crossing across three different points - Designed by Aditya Shinde only for representation purpose.Determining the shortest or most applicable route is not in the scope of this blog post. Every border crossing location is operated by an external party and charges vary depending on the location/Service provider. The manufacturer is entitled to pay an associated fee depending on multiple factors but here we will consider service provider and service location as the prime driver to identify payable amount.

Let’s move directly at an instance in time where a road FRO is created with 1 active stages with executing carrier along with Border crossing agent as the Additional agreement party. The truck will cross the border via Fort Erie

We have valid Service types, Freight agreement i.e. 1 for executing carrier and 1 for Border crossing agent.

We start with an assumption that Order management, planning, Charge management and settlement configs are in place as the focus is not on setting up the system but on functionality walk-through.

Pictorial representation of the demonstrated scenario:

Fort Erie border crossing point - Designed by Aditya Shinde only for representation purpose.Basic process flow (Representation purpose only) 

Step 1:  Road FRO is created with executing carrier determined.

 Step 2 : Border crossing agent BP added as Additional agreement party.

As mentioned earlier there are multiple ways to determine a BP for Additional agreement party. For demonstration purpose I have added the BP manually and executing/invoicing carriers may be determined subcontracting process.


Step 3: Once the carriers are determined, you can observe that a special service by the name ‘BC- Border crossing’ is assigned to the freight order.

 Important aspect to note here is that we have added 4 important values that play a pivotal role in triggering and performing charge calculation:

  1. Service type
  2. Check box (Item specific service provider)
  3. Service Location
  4. Duration.

 In the absence on the Check box (Item specific service provider), system will assume the service is performed by the executing carrier and will have to be maintained as part of executing carrier’s freight agreement and will get activated based on the associated service. There by payable to the executing carrier.

Once the above information is captured, you may save the freight order or execute charge calculation manually from the freight order.

Below is the result of the charge calculation.

 

Note: Charge structure and associated charges are for representation purpose and are fictitious in nature.


Step 4:  Freight order stages – Important observation here to make a note is that the stage of the freight order is unaltered here by reducing efforts to trace the border crossing location.

Step 5: Determining charge based on the border crossing location at stage level is possible by leveraging std calculation base.

 Here you can observe that the charge is determined via a rate table.

Rate table created having a calculation base as SRV_LOCATION which identifies the service location.

Alternatively, you can use SRV_DURATION – Service Duration as well to perform charge calculation.

In today’s demonstration, I have considered only service location based calculation.

The screenshot below demonstrates that system was able to initiate FSD creation for all of the applicable payable parties. 
 

The screenshot above demonstrates that no errors were observed during freight settlement document creation process and we can proceed with posting for accruals.
 
Note:
With the introduction of this functionality we can eliminate the introduction of service order which is an additional object and try to keep the process simple and reduce enhancement wherever applicable. Some exceptions may apply based on business scenarios and should be evaluated accordingly.

The demonstration in this blog post doesn’t focus on the configs to setup the system nor does it highlight all of the business rules associated with different industry sectors for the carrier payments.

Hope this article helps all of us understand and simplify the payment process associated with additional services incurred during transportation across various industries in the SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management world. There may be multiple different ways in which a contract may be negotiated between two parties, in this demonstration, I chose to keep it generic and simple.

Thank you for taking time and effort to read this.

Feedback/comments in any form will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Request to Duplicate Business Unit in SAP CDP
entry.content:
Duplication of Business units in sense to copy structured data at the moment is not supported in CDP.

In order to create same Business unit from existing Business unit there is option of import and export packages.

For example, your team can export structured data from Business unit PROD in JSON file and then import this file on Business unit DEV.

More details are available in documentation:

Import and export packages

KBA 3292363

entry.title: Re: Using HANA SQL Tabular Function in SQL Join
entry.content:
Dear @che_eky,
 
I did tests in my local system, HANA supports table function in JOIN.
Here are the steps and the SQLs I used during my test for your reference.
 
1. create a column store table: PRODUCTSALES
---------------------------------
CREATE ROW TABLE "I311040"."PRODUCTSALES" ( "PRODNAME" VARCHAR(50) CS_STRING,
"TYPE" VARCHAR(20) CS_STRING,
"SALES" INT CS_INT ) 
---------------------------------  
 
2. insert a few entries.
---------------------------------
PRODNAME;TYPE;SALES
Tee Shirt;Plain;21
Tee Shirt;Lettered;22
Tee Shirt;Team logo;30
Hoodie;Plain;60
Hoodie;Lettered;65
Hoodie;Team logo;80
Ballcap;Plain;8
Ballcap;Lettered;40
Ballcap;Team logo;27
---------------------------------
 
3. create table function.
---------------------------------
CREATE FUNCTION "I311040"."GET_ITEMS_BY_NAME" (
    IN name NVARCHAR(60)
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    PRODNAME NVARCHAR(60),
    SALES NVARCHAR(60)
)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
AS
BEGIN
    RETURN SELECT PRODNAME, SALES
    FROM PRODUCTSALES
    WHERE PRODNAME = :name;
END;
---------------------------------
 
4. JOIN the table function in SELECT statement.
---------------------------------
SELECT
    P.PRODNAME,
    I.SALES
FROM
    PRODUCTSALES P
JOIN
    "I311040"."GET_ITEMS_BY_NAME"('Hoodie') I
ON
    P.PRODNAME = I.PRODNAME;
---------------------------------
 
Result: see attachment "Charles Test result.png".
 
Hope it helps.
 
Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Re: SAP ChaRM - Configure the action to read the type of...
entry.content:
@miksoneee wrote:

Hi,

Could someone please tell me how I can configure the step/action, if transport = customizing, will automatically jump from step 3 (To Be Tested) to step 6 (Imported to QA) skipping the additional approvals required to import the transport to QA. (Step 4 - QA Approval Required, Step 5 - QA Movement Approved). I would like this step to check the type of transport and then decide if additional approvals will be required. If transport = workbench additional approvals will be required.

BR,
Szymon

To make Customizing transports (TRs) skip QA approval steps in SAP ChaRM, you need to configure your workflow to conditionally jump from "To Be Tested" (Step 3) directly to "Imported to QA" (Step 6). This is achieved by defining conditions, likely using BRFplus, that check the transport type: if it's a Customizing transport, the system bypasses the "QA Approval Required" (Step 4) and "QA Movement Approved" (Step 5) steps, while Workbench transports will still follow the full approval process. This involves setting up automated actions in ChaRM's status profile, linked to these BRFplus conditions, to trigger the direct import for Customizing TRs.

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Masashi Otsuka(sumikko)
entry.content:
Please scroll down for the English version.
This post has a lot of pictures so please take a look.

こんにちは、SAP Communityの皆さん！
河原哲也さん @tetsuyakawahara  からご紹介いただき、今回「Blog It Forward」に参加させていただくことになりました。
※Blog It Forwardのことは以前から知っていましたが、まさか自分に回ってくるとは思っていませんでした

この場を借りて、私のSAPとの関わりやコミュニティ活動についてご紹介させていただきます。

 

自己紹介

名前：大塚將司（Masashi Otsuka）
ニックネーム：すみっこ
役割：S/4HANA拡張領域のコンサルタント／開発者
SAP歴：ABAP開発から始まり、現在はSAP BTPやAWSを活用したソリューション開発に従事しています。

2024年には、SAPジャパンより「SAP BTP Champion」として表彰されました。
この賞は、SAP BTPを活用した革新的な取り組みや、コミュニティへの貢献が評価されたものです。

 


人生を変えた2つのきっかけ

私の人生が大きく変わったきっかけは、2つあります。

(1)ブログ「SAPのすみっこ」をはじめ、コミュニティ「chillSAP」を立ち上げたこと


技術的な知見やイベントの記録を発信する中で、思いがけず多くの方に読んでいただけるようになり、社内外から声をかけてもらう機会が増えました。書くことで自分の考えが整理され、発信することで新たな出会いが生まれ、「情報を発信すると情報が集まってくる」「発信は自分を変える力がある」と実感しました。

そして同じようにSNSで発信している人たちと「もっと自由に、もっと楽しくSAPを語れる場を作りたい」という思いから始めたchillSAP。
そこには、年齢も職種も企業も超えて、SAPに関わるあらゆる人が集まりました。
この活動を通じて、本当に多くの人とつながることができ、人生の景色が変わったと感じています。

「chillSAP」は、SAPに関わるすべての人が「ゆるく、楽しく、つながる」ことを目的とした日本発のコミュニティです。
2019年に設立し、現在までに多数のイベントを開催してきました。

イベント：SAPなんでもライトニングトーク祭
SAPに関する話題なら何でもOKの5分間のLT大会。自由な発表スタイルが特徴です。

 

SAP Inside Track Tokyoの開催
2019年からsitTokyoの運営にも携わっています。他国の詳細はあまり把握しきれていないですが、Tokyoはセッション数は世界でもトップクラスなのでは？と思っています。
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2019 finished a great success #Recap #sitTokyo 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2023 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2024 イベントサイト 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information 

過去動画もYoutubeで公開していますので、ぜひご覧ください。

ちなみに、私もたまに登壇しています。2025年は「ABAP developer capabilities with Joule」をテーマにお話しさせていだきました。よろしければご覧ください。

 

(2)SAP TechEdに参加して刺激的な学びと出会いがあったこと

SAP TechEdは、私にとって技術と人とのつながりを再確認する場です。これまでに複数回参加し、毎回多くの刺激を受けてきました。

2018年
TechEd Las Vegasにブロガーとして初参加
ブログを始めてわずか8か月で、SAPジャパンから招待されて参加。
「まさか自分が」と驚きつつも、現地での体験がその後のキャリアと人生を大きく変えるきっかけに。

2019年
TechEd Las Vegasで「勝手にすみっこNight」開催
自主的に日本人参加者を集めて交流会を開催。企業や立場を超えてつながる場を自ら作り出した経験は、コミュニティ活動の原点のひとつです

2023年
TechEd Bangaloreに現地参加
コロナ後初のオンサイト開催。SAP BuildやAI関連セッションの実践的なデモや現地参加者の熱量に感動。
世界中の開発者と直接交流できたことが、技術的にも人間的にも大きな刺激に。



日本で開催されたSAP TechEd Japanのクロージングセッションでパネリストとして登壇させていただきました

 

2024年
TechEd on Tour @メルボルン（SAUG-x 2024）
空いていて快適な環境で、じっくりとセッションに集中することができました。
SAPジャパンによる日本人向けサポートも充実しており、安心して参加できた。

 

 

 

 

TechEdを通じたモチベーションの再充電
「これがあるから1年頑張れる」と思うほど、TechEdは私にとって心のチャージの場。
SNSやYouTubeで見ていたSAP Communityの有名な皆さんたちのセッションを生で体験できたことは、今でも忘れられません。
私と写真を撮った記憶がある方はいますか？🙋

 

 

 

BIF質問への回答

自分が20歳に戻ったら何を勉強したい？
英語とIT基礎です。これらをしっかりと学ぶべきだったと思っています(いま勉強中です)

両親から学んだベストレッスン
機嫌のいい両親から学んだことは、「自分の機嫌は自分でとる」ということです。

 

次のBlog It Forward参加者

私からは、日本で一緒にコミュニティを運営しているみのさん( @Mino )  にバトンを渡したいと思います。みのさんの知的好奇心の持ち方と信念がブレないところに私は常に刺激をもらっています。
みのさんの BIF ブログを、ぜひお楽しみに！

 

最後に

SAPのすみっこブログから発信を始めて、今では多くの方とつながることができました。これからもコミュニティを盛り上げていきたいと思います。Blog It Forwardにも参加できて光栄です。
ここまで読んでいただき、ありがとうございました！

以下は、私が2018年に初めて社外で登壇した際の写真です




--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello, SAP Community!

Thanks to the introduction from Mr. Tetsuya Kawahara @tetsuyakawahara  , I’m excited to be participating in the Blog It Forward  initiative.

I’ve known about Blog It Forward for a while, but I never imagined it would come around to me!

I’d like to take this opportunity to share a bit about my involvement with SAP and my activities within the community.

 

About me

Name: Masashi Otsuka
Nickname: Sumikko
Role: Consultant/Developer in the S/4HANA Extension Area
SAP Experience: I started with ABAP development and am currently engaged in solution development utilizing SAP BTP and AWS.

In 2024, I was honored by SAP Japan as an SAP BTP Champion.
This award recognizes innovative initiatives leveraging SAP BTP and contributions to the community.

 


Two Turning Points That Changed My Life

There are two major turning points that significantly changed the course of my life.

(1) Starting the blog “SAP Sumikko” and launching the community “chillSAP”

 

While sharing technical insights and event recaps through my blog, I was pleasantly surprised to find that many people started reading it. This led to more opportunities to connect with others, both inside and outside my company. Writing helped me organize my thoughts, and sharing them brought new encounters. I came to truly realize that “sharing information attracts more information” and “sharing has the power to transform you.”

Inspired by others who were also actively sharing on social media, I launched chillSAP with the desire to create a space where people could talk about SAP more freely and enjoyably.

chillSAP brought together people from all backgrounds—regardless of age, profession, or company—united by their connection to SAP. Through this activity, I’ve been able to connect with so many people, and I feel that the entire landscape of my life has changed.

chillSAP is a Japan-born community with the goal of helping everyone involved with SAP connect in a relaxed and enjoyable way.
Founded in 2019, we’ve hosted numerous events to date.

Event Highlight: SAP Anything Lightning Talk Festival
A 5-minute lightning talk event where any SAP-related topic is welcome.
The presentations are characterized by their free and flexible style, allowing participants to share ideas in a relaxed and creative way.

 

Event Highlight: SAP Inside Track Tokyo

Since 2019, I’ve been involved in organizing SAP Inside Track Tokyo (sitTokyo).
Although I’m not fully familiar with the details of other countries’ events, I believe Tokyo ranks among the top globally in terms of the number of sessions.
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2019 finished a great success #Recap #sitTokyo 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2023 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2024 イベントサイト 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information 

We’ve also published recordings of past sessions on YouTube, so please feel free to check them out!
Youtube

By the way, I also occasionally speak at events.
In 2025, I gave a talk on the theme “ABAP Developer Capabilities with Joule.”
Please feel free to check it out if you're interested!
YouTube

 

(2) Attending SAP TechEd and Experiencing Inspiring Learning and Encounters

SAP TechEd has always been a place for me to reconnect with both technology and people.
I’ve attended multiple times, and each time has brought me fresh inspiration and motivation.

2018
I attended TechEd Las Vegas for the first time as a blogger.
Just eight months after starting my blog, I was invited by SAP Japan to join the event.
I was honestly surprised—“I can’t believe it’s me!”—but the experience on-site became a major turning point in both my career and my life.

2019
At TechEd Las Vegas, I hosted an informal meetup called “Sumikko Night.”
I took the initiative to gather Japanese participants and create a space for connection and conversation.
This experience of building a community space that transcended companies and roles became one of the foundations of my community activities.

2023
I attended TechEd Bangalore in person—the first on-site TechEd after the COVID-19 pandemic.
I was deeply impressed by the practical demos on SAP Build and AI-related sessions, as well as the energy and enthusiasm of the local participants.
Being able to directly interact with developers from around the world was incredibly stimulating, both technically and personally.



I also had the honor of speaking as a panelist during the closing session of SAP TechEd Japan, held in Tokyo.

2024
I participated in TechEd on Tour @ Melbourne (SAUG-x 2024).
The venue was spacious and comfortable, allowing me to fully concentrate on the sessions.
SAP Japan provided excellent support for Japanese attendees, which made the experience smooth and reassuring.

 

 

 

 

Recharging My Motivation Through TechEd

TechEd is a place that recharges my spirit—so much so that I feel, “This is what keeps me going for the year.”
Being able to attend sessions by well-known SAP Community members, whom I had only seen on social media or YouTube, was an unforgettable experience.

It was incredibly inspiring to meet these people in person and feel the energy of the global SAP community.
TechEd reminds me of the joy of learning and the power of connection—it’s a source of motivation that fuels me throughout the year.

Do any of you remember taking a photo with me? 🙋

 

 

 

Answers to Blog It Forward Questions

 

If you could go back to age 20, what would you study?
I would study English and IT fundamentals.
I believe I should have learned these properly back then—I'm currently studying them now!

Best lesson learned from your parents?
From my cheerful parents, I learned this valuable lesson:
“Take care of your own mood.”

 

Next Blog It Forward Participant

I’d like to pass the baton to Mino-san ( @Mino ), who co-manages the community with me here in Japan.
I’m always inspired by his intellectual curiosity and unwavering sense of purpose.
Please look forward to his Blog It Forward post!

 

In Closing

Starting with my blog SAP Sumikko, I’ve been fortunate to connect with many wonderful people.
I hope to continue contributing to and energizing the SAP community.
It’s truly an honor to be part of the Blog It Forward initiative.

Thank you so much for reading all the way through!

Below is a photo from my very first external speaking engagement in 2018.

entry.title: Clarification on UAE WPS Payment Processing Based on Sponsor ID
entry.content:
Dear Experts

We would appreciate your expertise, help, and support regarding the WPS processing configuration as per UAE regulations and company Group’s internal payroll model. Kindly take note of the following key requirements:

  • In the case of WPS payments, Company Group processes salary disbursements through sponsor-specific bank accounts, rather than the legal entity’s house bank.
  • Each sponsor is linked to a specific bank account, and a single legal entity may have multiple associated sponsors.
  • Therefore, WPS files must be generated based on the Sponsor ID, and not solely by company code.
  • If the Sponsor ID is the same as the Legal Entity ID, then the default house bank for the company code should be used.
  • For example, if a specific business unit has 100 employees across 5 different sponsors, the system should generate 5 separate WPS files, one for each sponsor along with sponsor bank details 
  • Sponsor bank account details are already maintained in the system to support this logic.

Summary of Required Logic:

  • Use Sponsor ID as the basis for WPS file generation.
  • Usi the Company Code’s house bank only when Sponsor ID = Legal Entity ID.

Thanks

Sameer

entry.title: Redesigned Applicant Workbench - "View Profile" Button Now Available!
entry.content:
We're Excited to Bring Back a Favorite Feature in the Redesigned Applicant Workbench!
Thanks to your valuable feedback, we’re pleased to announce a new update to the Redesigned Applicant Workbench—the return of a highly requested feature from the legacy workbench.

The "View Profile" deep link is back!
You can now easily access a candidate’s profile directly from the application detail page, making your navigation smoother and your recruiting workflow more efficient.

This enhancement was driven by customer input through the Influence Portal, where more than 80 customers voted to bring this feature back. We heard you—and we delivered.

Stay tuned for more updates, and thank you for helping us improve your recruiting experience!

Helpful Resources

  • Supercharge Your Hiring with the New Applicant Workbench

  • SAP Help Guide: The Redesigned Applicant Management Tool

  • KBA 3369122: Redesigned Applicant Management Workbench - Recruiting Management

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
